artifacts.content:
Hi Izzy,

I finally have a quiet minute at Mom’s dining table — she’s napping and the IV pump is actually behaving for once — so I wanted to give you a fuller download on how things are going and what the next couple of months might look like.

You already saw the email I sent HR about working remotely from 3/9–4/19 so I can stay here in Albuquerque and juggle Insight360 backend work with Mom’s meds, PT, and the drives back and forth to Presbyterian. Karen wrote back yesterday and formally approved the temporary remote arrangement, with the usual conditions about keeping 9:00–5:30 MT hours, staying on Slack, and doing a check‑in with her and Daniel around 3/29. I replied confirming I’m good with all of it and mentioned I’d be blocking off little windows for Mom’s appointments and paperwork.

In some ways this feels like a dry run for what we might be dealing with long‑term. I keep thinking about how we handled things after Dad’s heart attack in 2021 and how different it would be if something like a stroke or cancer hit Mom later on. I know you’ve got your hands full in Seattle, especially after Liam’s scare at Children’s last year, but I’m glad we’re getting practice coordinating care across time zones.

Day to day it’s a lot of small stuff: grocery runs, re‑labeling her pill boxes, helping her into the shower, and then trying to shift my brain back into code review mode before the 9:15 stand‑up. Alicia has been a lifesaver on the heavier PT days, coming over so I can stay heads‑down during sprint planning.

How are Liam and Sophie doing? Send me a couple of pictures when you get a chance — Mom perks up every time she sees their faces on my phone.

Love,
Michael

Karen,

Thank you again for the thoughtful response and for working with Daniel to get the temporary remote arrangement in place. I’m writing just to formally confirm that I understand and accept all of the terms you outlined for the 2026‑03‑09 through 2026‑04‑19 period.

I’ll be based at my mom Elena’s condo at 415 Lomas Blvd NE here in Albuquerque. IT overnighted my Horizon laptop and a replacement YubiKey to that address after I misplaced the original token back at my Denver apartment last month, and everything is set up in a quiet spare bedroom so I can work uninterrupted.

I’ll maintain my standard Monday–Friday 9:00 a.m.–5:30 p.m. MT schedule with a 30‑minute lunch, stay reachable on Slack, email, and my cell, and continue to participate fully in stand‑ups, sprint ceremonies, and one‑on‑ones with Daniel. For transparency, I’ve already shared my Google Calendar with him and added holds for a few recurring care items for my mom (e.g., her follow‑up at Presbyterian and some Medicaid re‑certification paperwork I need to handle on Wednesdays).

I’m also keeping an eye on my own health in the middle of all this. I have a primary care appointment at Denver Health on 2026‑03‑06 to check in about the anxiety medication I’ve been on since my dad’s stroke back in 2019, and I’ve blocked that off as out‑of‑office time.

I’m fine with the mid‑period check‑in around 2026‑03‑29 with you and Daniel to make sure this is working for everyone.

Best regards,

Michael J. Ramirez
Senior Software Engineer, Insight360
Horizon Data Systems

metadata.subject: Confirmation of temporary remote work terms

artifacts.content:
Title: Caregiving + work logistics – March–April 2026

Location: Mom’s house – spare bedroom “office”

1. Work schedule / commitments
- Stick to 9:00–5:30 MT, daily 9:15 stand‑up, plus sprint planning/retro.
- Keep Slack status accurate when driving Mom to Presbyterian or dealing with home health.
- Weekly Sunday night email to Daniel summarizing Insight360 backend progress, code reviews, and any blockers.

2. Mom’s medical / admin to‑dos
- IV antibiotics schedule posted on fridge; set phone alarms for each infusion.
- PT at Presbyterian Hospital three times a week (Mon/Wed/Fri) – coordinate around meetings.
- Wednesdays in March–April: block 1.5–2 hours to handle her Medicaid re‑certification and calls about the $4,800 oncology bill from her January 2026 UNM Hospital admission; confirm payment plan details.
- Ask her doctor again about fall‑risk precautions; make sure she’s not left alone at night.

3. Family coordination
- Text Alicia weekly with a simple schedule so she knows which days I really can’t step away from Zoom.
- Regular video check‑ins with Isabel; keep her looped in on Mom’s mobility, mood, and any big changes.
- Start a shared Google Doc for longer‑term planning (what happens if Mom needs more help after April).

4. My own health
- 2026‑03‑06: Denver Health PCP follow‑up re: anxiety meds; ask about sleep and on‑call stress.
- If insomnia gets worse, consider asking Daniel about staying remote a bit longer and shifting some on‑call to Priya.

5. Reminders
- Don’t try to “make up” caregiving time by working late every night.
- It’s okay to ask HR for another check‑in if things get overwhelming.

Event: Remote from Mom’s house – support her recovery (meds, PT, Presbyterian follow‑ups)

Dates: All‑day, 2026‑03‑09 through 2026‑04‑19
Location: Elena’s house – 415 Lomas Blvd NE, Albuquerque, NM
Attendees: Michael (primary); FYI access for Daniel Cho

Description:

Working remotely full‑time from Mom’s place for six weeks while she recovers from the post‑operative infection after her knee replacement. She was admitted to Presbyterian Hospital on 2026‑02‑24 and discharged home with IV antibiotics and intensive PT.

Plan is to keep my normal Horizon Data Systems schedule (9:00 a.m.–5:30 p.m. MT), join all Insight360 stand‑ups, sprint ceremonies, and code reviews via Zoom, and lean on Priya for any on‑site hardware checks.

Caregiving blocks to keep in mind:
- PT at Presbyterian three times a week (separate recurring events on Mon/Wed/Fri).
- Wednesday admin window (1.5–2 hours) for Mom’s Medicaid re‑certification and UNM Hospital $4,800 oncology bill payment plan calls.
- Evenings mostly reserved for Mom’s exercises and making sure she’s not alone given fall‑risk.

Notes to self:
- Use this event to remind myself not to overbook late‑night on‑call while juggling Mom’s IV schedule.
- HR check‑in with Karen + Daniel around 2026‑03‑29 to review how this arrangement is working.
- If Mom’s mobility is still poor by mid‑April, start drafting request for an extension and talk to Isabel and Alicia about longer‑term rotation.

metadata.title: Remote from Mom’s house – support her recovery

artifacts.content:
Hey Priya,

Just wanted to give you a bit more context since you’ve been so generous about covering on‑site stuff while I’m here in Albuquerque.

As you know, Mom’s been on IV antibiotics and doing PT at Presbyterian three times a week since the post‑surgical infection they caught on 2/24. HR signed off on me working from her place from 3/9–4/19, and I promised Karen and Daniel I’d keep normal 9–5:30 hours and stay fully plugged into the Insight360 backend work.

The tricky part is the edges of the day. I’m trying to limit my overnight or extended on‑call shifts during this window because I’m the only one here to manage her IV schedule and get her to PT. I told Daniel I can still take daytime incident rotations, but if there’s a choice on the really late‑night pages, it’d help a ton if they skewed toward you or the others for now.

On top of that, I’ve got a Denver Health PCP appointment on 3/6 to tweak my anxiety meds (been on them since my dad’s stroke back in 2019), so I’m trying to be honest about my own bandwidth. Between that and the Wednesday admin blocks for Mom’s Medicaid re‑cert and the UNM Hospital oncology bill, my calendar is starting to look like a game of Tetris.

I really appreciate you stepping in for any random “can someone plug this into the rack” requests at the office. Once I’m back in Denver, I owe you many coffees and probably a week of taking the pager so you can sleep.

– Michael

metadata.subject: On-call / coverage while I’m in Albuquerque

artifacts.content:
Hi Mom,

I know we’re in the same house, but you always say you like having things in writing to look back on, so here’s the plan for the next few weeks while I’m working from your place.

From March 9 through April 19 I’ll be doing my normal Horizon schedule (9:00 a.m.–5:30 p.m.) from the spare bedroom. I’ve already told my HR manager, Karen, and my boss, Daniel, that I’m here in Albuquerque to help you with the IV antibiotics, PT at Presbyterian, and all the follow‑ups from that infection they found after your surgery on 2/24.

During the day:
- I’ll come out and check on you between meetings and at lunch.
- On PT days (Monday, Wednesday, Friday), we’ll leave a little early so we’re not rushing to Presbyterian. I’ve blocked those times on my calendar so people don’t book me.
- On Wednesdays, I’ll need about 1.5–2 hours to call UNM Hospital about the $4,800 oncology bill from January and to work on your Medicaid re‑certification paperwork. I’ll do that from the dining room so you can hear what they say.

Evenings are mostly for you: dinner, exercises, getting you settled so you’re not alone when you’re unsteady. I told Alicia and Isabel that we’ll keep reevaluating as we get closer to April in case you need more help overnight.

If you ever feel like I’m “too busy with work,” please tell me. The whole point of me being here is so you’re not going through this by yourself.

Love,
Michael

metadata.subject: Our plan for the next six weeks

artifacts.content:
Title: Longer‑term caregiving scenarios – brain dump

Sitting in the spare bedroom after the 9:15 stand‑up, trying to think past the next IV infusion.

1. If Mom’s recovery stalls
- If by the 3/29 HR check‑in she still needs hands‑on help for basic stuff (transfers, showering), I may need to ask Karen and Daniel about extending the remote period past 4/19.
- That will mean re‑negotiating on‑call with Priya so I’m not doing overnight pages on top of nighttime supervision.

2. What if there’s another major event?
- Keep flashing back to Dad’s stroke in 2019 and how unprepared we were. Part of me worries about something like a stroke or cancer hitting Mom later this year.
- If that happened, I’d probably move here longer‑term, work fully remote, and rotate nights with Alicia and maybe Isabel flying in for stretches.

3. Money / paperwork
- Don’t forget the UNM Hospital $4,800 oncology bill and Medicaid re‑cert — those are canaries in the coal mine for how complicated this could get.
- Need a shared spreadsheet with Isabel for tracking bills, insurance appeals, and who called which office when.

4. My own limits
- PCP at Denver Health on 3/6 to talk meds and insomnia.
- If Dr. Kim at Denver Behavioral Health Clinic has availability in April/May, consider starting biweekly 45‑minute sessions to head off burnout.

5. Career thoughts
- I like Horizon and the Insight360 work, but I need to be honest with myself (and Daniel) if caregiving becomes a permanent part of my life.

From: Michael James Ramirez <mramirez@horizondatasystems.com>
To: Daniel Cho <dcho@horizondatasystems.com>; Karen L. Whitfield <kwhitfield@horizondatasystems.com>
Cc: Priya Patel <ppatel@horizondatasystems.com>
Date: 2026-03-04
Subject: Consolidated remote work/leave requests for upcoming family medical needs

Hi Daniel, hi Karen,

I wanted to put everything in one place so we can plan my schedule and any temporary remote assignments clearly.

1) In the immediate term, I need a temporary remote assignment starting 2026-03-09 while caring for my mom, Elena Martinez Ramirez, after her March 2026 ischemic stroke and inpatient rehabilitation at St. Joseph Hospital in Denver. She’ll be discharged to my apartment at 742 W 32nd Avenue, Apt 304, and will need post-discharge home care. I’m requesting temporary WFH so I can be her primary caregiver during this phase.

2) I also need remote work authorization during April–May 2026 while I coordinate on-site coverage with Priya Patel and attend biweekly 45-minute appointments with Dr. Leonard Kim, my psychiatrist at Denver Behavioral Health Clinic, for stress-related insomnia treatment. I’ll remain fully engaged with the Insight360 backend work while Priya handles most on-site needs at the Horizon Data Systems downtown Denver office.

3) Looking slightly ahead, I may need a further remote period March–June 2026 to stay at Alicia’s apartment near Sloan’s Lake, Denver, CO as primary caregiver for my maternal aunt, Alicia Martinez, during her Stage III breast cancer chemotherapy treatment plan at Denver Health Medical Center. I’d still work my normal hours; I’d just need flexibility around her chemo days.

Happy to discuss details or adjust sprint responsibilities so this is manageable for the team.

Best,
Michael

metadata.subject: Consolidated remote work/leave requests for upcoming family medical needs

artifacts.content:
From: Michael James Ramirez <mramirez@horizondatasystems.com>
To: Karen L. Whitfield <kwhitfield@horizondatasystems.com>
Date: 2026-03-06
Subject: Family medical emergency – temporary leave and remote status confirmations

Karen,

Following up on our conversation, I need to formally request temporary release from my current assignment starting 2026-03-09 because my mother, Elena Ramirez, was hospitalized at Presbyterian Hospital, Albuquerque, NM on 2026-02-24 with a post-surgical infection and now requires round-the-clock supervision at home for at least six weeks while she completes IV antibiotics and intensive physical therapy. I’ll be relocating from the Incident Command Post (ICP) to her place in Albuquerque and will continue remote work as much as possible while providing in-home assistance during her IV antibiotic infusions and early-stage post-operative PT, which began after the knee replacement–related infection treated at Presbyterian Hospital last week.

I’ve read your draft of the temporary remote work arrangement from 2026-03-09 through 2026-04-19 and I confirm that I understand and accept all the terms, including the mid-period check-in around 2026-03-29. I’ll be working out of my mother Elena Martinez Ramirez’s apartment in Albuquerque and taking her to oncology appointments at Presbyterian Hospital every Tuesday and Thursday at 10:30 a.m. for Stage III breast cancer chemo infusions, which is why I requested flexibility around those specific times.

Just so expectations are clear, if I seem distracted some afternoons it may be because I’m coordinating with my aunt Alicia Martinez and my sister Isabel Ramirez Chen about rotating overnight care for Mom after her April 2026 lumpectomy at Lovelace Medical Center, Albuquerque, NM, scheduled for 2026-04-03 and likely requiring me to sleep at her residence for 3–4 nights.

Best regards,
Michael

metadata.subject: Family medical emergency – temporary leave and remote status confirmations

artifacts.content:
From: Michael James Ramirez <michael.ramirez@gmail.com>
To: Isabel Ramirez Chen <isabel.chen@example.com>
Cc: Elena Martinez Ramirez <elena.ramirez@example.com>
Date: 2026-04-10
Subject: Remote work plans – Liam, counseling, and helping Tía Alicia

Izzy,

Wanted to loop you and Mom in on how I’m juggling work with everything coming up.

First, about Liam: after his April 2026 hospitalization at Children’s Hospital Colorado in Aurora for diabetic ketoacidosis and his Type 1 diabetes diagnosis, I told my manager that I’m willing to get remote status approved for a few weeks so I can stay at the Chen family home in Aurora, CO and supervise his post-discharge insulin regimen and daily routine. I made it clear I’d still be working full-time; I just need to be physically there while you and David are at work.

At the same time, I finally asked HR for adjusted schedule/remote days so I can attend weekly 60-minute family grief counseling sessions at the Denver Family Services counseling center, Denver, CO. With Dad, Carlos Ramirez, gone since 05/14/2021, the therapist thinks weekly sessions in Spring 2026 will help all of us process the ongoing grief.

On top of that, from March–June 2026 I’m planning to request remote work authorization to stay at Alicia’s apartment near Sloan’s Lake so I can be primary caregiver for Tía Alicia during her Stage III breast cancer chemotherapy treatment plan at Denver Health Medical Center, Denver, CO. I’ll handle rides to chemo, meds, and meals, similar to what I’m doing with Mom.

It’s a lot, but I’d rather be the one shuffling my calendar than let you carry all of this alone.

Love,
Michael

metadata.subject: Remote work plans – Liam, counseling, and helping Tía Alicia

artifacts.content:
From: Michael James Ramirez <mramirez@horizondatasystems.com>
To: Daniel Cho <dcho@horizondatasystems.com>, Karen L. Whitfield <kwhitfield@horizondatasystems.com>
Date: 2026-03-04 08:17 MT
Subject: Consolidated remote work requests for upcoming family medical and counseling needs

Daniel, Karen,

I wanted to outline a few situations coming up this spring where I’ll need some flexibility around remote work and location, so we can plan coverage and expectations for Insight360.

1) Mom – stroke and rehab (March 2026)
My mother, **Elena Martinez Ramirez**, had an ischemic stroke earlier in **March 2026** and is currently in inpatient rehabilitation at **St. Joseph Hospital in Denver**. Once she’s discharged, she’ll be at my apartment at **742 W 32nd Avenue, Apt 304, Denver, CO** for post-discharge home care. I’m requesting a **temporary remote assignment** while caring for her during this rehab and home recovery period so I can manage medications, appointments, and safety at home.

2) Grief counseling after Dad’s death
Since my dad, **Carlos Ramirez**, passed away on **05/14/2021**, my family and I have been doing ongoing grief work. I’ve recently started **weekly 60-minute family counseling sessions in Spring 2026** at **Denver Family Services counseling center**. I’m requesting that my schedule be adjusted so I can work remotely on those days and reliably attend these standing weekly therapy appointments.

3) Insomnia treatment and on-site coverage
I’ve also been diagnosed with **stress-related insomnia** and have begun treatment with **Dr. Leonard Kim**, a psychiatrist at **Denver Behavioral Health Clinic**. I’ll have **biweekly 45-minute appointments during April–May 2026**. During **April–May 2026**, I’d like to keep a **remote work** arrangement most days while **Priya Patel** continues to handle on-site coordination at the Horizon downtown Denver office.

I’ll maintain my usual availability, keep up with the on-call rotation as feasible, and coordinate carefully with Priya and the rest of the team.

Thanks for considering this,
Michael

metadata.subject: Consolidated remote work requests for upcoming family medical and counseling needs

Daniel,

I wanted to give you a clearer picture of my caregiving responsibilities over the next few months and how I’m planning to keep work on track.

This essentially formalizes what we discussed about a **six-week remote period** while I’m the only nearby family member available.

Related to that, I’ve already submitted a separate request to the **Incident Command Post (ICP)** for **non-urgent transport support** to relocate from the ICP to my mother **Elena Ramirez’s** home in **Albuquerque** on **2026-03-09**, so I can continue remote work while assisting with her **IV antibiotic infusions** and early-stage post-operative physical therapy following the knee replacement–related infection treated at **Presbyterian Hospital** last week.

Because her physician at **Presbyterian Hospital, Albuquerque, NM** has advised she not be left alone due to fall risk and complications from the **2026-02-24 post-operative infection**, I may have **intermittent unavailability during evening operational periods over the next six weeks**. I’ll block those times on my calendar and keep Priya in the loop so she can cover any urgent on-site needs.

I’ll send weekly status updates and remain fully engaged with stand-ups, code reviews, and incident response during core hours.

Thanks,
Michael

artifacts.content:
Thread: Family – Mom’s Treatment
Participants: Michael, Isabel
Date: Sun, 9 Mar 2026, 20:41 MT

Michael: Hey, quick update before I crash. HR finally wrapped everything up on Friday.

Michael: Karen, I confirm that I understand and accept all the terms of the temporary remote work arrangement from 2026-03-09 through 2026-04-19. That exact wording had to go in my email, lol.

Michael: I told her I’ll be working out of Mom’s apartment in Albuquerque so I can take her to her oncology appointments at Presbyterian Hospital, Albuquerque, New Mexico every Tuesday and Thursday at 10:30 a.m.

Michael: I also spelled out that they’re Stage III breast cancer chemo infusions so she’d understand why I requested the flexibility around those specific times on my calendar.

Isabel: Ugh, seeing “Stage III breast cancer chemo infusions” written out like that still hits hard. But I’m glad your company is being reasonable.

Michael: Yeah, Horizon’s been decent. Daniel and Priya already shuffled stand-up and my heavier meetings away from those slots.

Michael: I’ll send you a screenshot of Mom’s oncology schedule and the remote work approval tomorrow so we’re all looking at the same dates.

Isabel: Thanks. I hate that I can’t be there with you, but knowing you’re in ABQ and at Presbyterian with her on Tue/Thu helps a lot.

Michael: We’ll tag-team this. You keep doing the emotional support over FaceTime; I’ve got the driving and chemo days.
